expectations.text: Report opens with 3-5 sentences of prose feedback for the professor
expectations.evidence: Opens with Section 1 'Reference Verification' — a data table. Narrative verdict is in Section 8 at the end. Professor must read 7 sections of tables before reaching actionable conclusion.

expectations.text: Report includes depth rubric with 8 dimensions, numeric scores, AND implication column
expectations.evidence: Uses ad-hoc 7-dimension quality framework (Section 6) with VERIFIED/EXACT MATCH/CORRECT ratings, not D1-D8 numeric 10-point rubric with implication column.

expectations.text: Report applies GRADE to at least 1 clinical claim with specific reasoning (not N/A)
expectations.evidence: Section 7 'If GRADE Were Applied': full 5-domain assessment. MACCE: MODERATE (RoB + indirectness). Bleeding: HIGH imprecision. With domain-by-domain rationale.

expectations.text: Report analyzes the slide's position in the narrative arc
expectations.evidence: Section 5: 'slide 14 of 18, first application slide (Phase F3) after methodology in F2'. Strength #3 identifies cognitive tension from GRADE gap. Discusses what residents need at this stage.

expectations.text: Report cross-references evidence-db against slide HTML
expectations.evidence: Section 4: 11-field cross-reference table (Autores, Journal, PMID, Design, N, Follow-up, HR, CI, GRADE, CYP2C19, NICE gap) with Present/Match/Notes columns.

expectations.text: Report suggests changes in prose explaining WHY they matter
expectations.evidence: Weaknesses W1-W4 each with 'Recommendation:' and reasoning. W1: 'ARR and NNT are more actionable than HR for residents learning clinical decision-making.'

expectations.text: Report includes synthesis across legs with interpreted convergences
expectations.evidence: Single evaluator approach, no parallel legs. No cross-source synthesis or convergence analysis. All checks done sequentially by one agent.
